course_description:
Intermediate study of Western classical music ensemble preparation and performance

Students taking this course participate in and are assessed on their work in one of three different ensemble options:

Each option has a different application process, and students are encouraged to provisionally enrol in the course while going through the application process. If you are not successful in your application, you must either apply for another option or drop your enrolment in the course.

The application processes will be advised in Blackboard prior to Orientation week.

This course is not available for Bachelor of Music Students; please enrol in MUSC2755 instead.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:
incompatible: You can't enrol in this course if you've already completed the following:
restrictions: Enrolment is subject to successful audition for a place in a large ensemble

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
Students in this course are expected to attend all classes, rehearsals and performances for the ensemble on which they successfully auditioned. Any requests for planned absences must be provided in writing to the relevant teacher(s) and course coordinator well in advance, and medical certificates for unplanned absences must be provided as soon as possible following the period of absence.
course_aims: This intermediateﾠlevelﾠcourse develops your capacity and ability to rehearse and perform as part of an ensemble.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate your understanding of effective collaborative approaches to the preparation of ensemble repertoire for performance at an intermediate level.
Identify and overcome challenges in the preparation of individual parts in a manner that supports being an effective ensemble performer at an intermediate level
Demonstrate your awareness of professional expectations surrounding ensemble performance in varied contexts at an intermediate level

assessment_details.title: Performance Report/Director's Report
assessment_details.category: Participation/ Student contribution, Performance, Practical/ Demonstration
assessment_details.due_date: Week 7 Mon - Week 13 Sun Precise times of assessment activities will be communicated via Blackboard
assessment_details.task_description: At the completion of the activities for the semester a final report for UQSO, UQWS and UQ Chorale members is provided by the Ensemble Director or UQ assessor. Rubrics or marking criteria will be made available on Blackboard before the start of semester. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Not a submissible item, assessment is performance based throughout the second half of semester.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
An extension/deferral is not possible as assessment is based on participation and engagement with group learning activities undertaken throughout the semester.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessment_details.title: Performance Report/Director's Report
assessment_details.category: Participation/ Student contribution, Performance, Practical/ Demonstration
assessment_details.due_date: Week 1 - Week 6 Fri Precise times of assessment activities will be communicated via Blackboard
assessment_details.task_description: Students will receive an Interim assessment comprising 40% of the assessment for this course. Rubrics or marking criteria will be made available on Blackboard before the start of semester. For UQ Chorale, this is provided by the ensemble leader no later than the end of Week 6. For UQ Symphony Orchestra, this assessment will be practical sectionals. A report form will be provided to the student by the end of Week 6. For UQ Wind Symphony , this assessment will be practical sectionals. A report form will be provided to the student by the end of Week 6. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .
learning_resources.other_course_materials:
If we've listed something under further requirement, you'll need to provide your own. Required Item Description Further Requirement Hearing Protection Students and staff participating in some practical activities are exposed to hazardous noise, and must have appropriate hearing protection to reduce the level of exposure so it is safe to participate in those activities. Students must obtain and use approved hearing protection to participate in these classes.

Current approved options are:
- custom fitted musician moulds with at least 10dB attenuation purchased at the UQ Health and Rehabilitation Clinics through the Audiology Clinic.
- Etymotic ER20 ETY High Fidelity Ear Plugs

Other hearing protection must be reviewed by the Health, Safety and Wellness Coordinator before being approved for use.

Additional activities may be identified during the semester as a result of our ongoing testing processes, and announced on Blackboard. own item needed Instruments Students are expected to bring their own instruments to all ensemble activities as advised. Students who are either required or invited to participate in any ensemble activity are expected to bring their own instrument to rehearsals and performances, with the following exceptions where the School will provide the instruments:
cor anglais
bass clarinet
contra-bassoon
percussion own item needed Scores A range of public domain and copyrighted materials will be required for the various elements of this course. Music scores for choral works may have to be purchased, hired or downloaded. Orchestral scores will be provided. Chamber Music scores can be hired from UQ library or accessed through public domain. Please see Blackboard for details. own item needed

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13
learning_activities.topic: UQ Chorale Rehearsals and Performances All students in this ensemble are expected to attend regular timetabled weekly rehearsals, any additional rehearsals that are scheduled in the lead-up to performances, and those performances. Dates and times for additional rehearsals and performances will be announced on Blackboard as the semester progresses. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13
learning_activities.topic: Private Practice All streams of activity in this course require diligent individual practice and preparation (including non-teaching weeks). Learning outcomes: L02, L03

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13
learning_activities.topic: UQ Wind Symphony Rehearsals and Performances All students who successfully auditioned for this ensemble are expected to practice and prepare parts to a high level for rehearsals and performances. Students are expected to attend all rehearsals. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

UQ Symphony Orchestra Rehearsals and Performances All students who successfully auditioned for this ensemble are expected to practice and prepare parts to a high level leading up to the start of intensive activity focused in and around Project Week. Due to the intensive nature of study in this stream (there are no timetabled weekly rehearsals), students must commit to attend all rehearsals without exception. Most rehearsals will take place after hours between 6.30-9.30pm. All of the evenings of Project Week should be reserved for this activity in addition to the weekend of the performance. The final timetable for rehearsals will be finalised by the end of Week 4 of semester. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . School guidelines Your school has additional guidelines you'll need to follow for this course: School of Music site Health and Wellbeing for Musicians Learning activities
